panels.description: Medium close-up of Elizabeth, her posture stiff and face flushed with repressed anger, eyes sharp and fixed on Darcy. Victorian drawing room background with soft ambient lighting.

bubbles.text: You are mistaken, Mr. Darcy, if you suppose your declaration affected me in any way other than sparing me the concern I might have felt in refusing you.

panels.description: Side profile view: Elizabeth gesturing firmly while speaking, Darcy stands slightly back, his face showing a flicker of shocked realization and suppressed hurt. Victorian wallpaper in the background.

bubbles.text: Your arrogance and conceit have built an immovable dislike in me. You are the last man in the world I could ever be prevailed on to marry.

panels.description: Close-up of Darcy's face, cold and mask-like, eyes narrowed in hurt, before he turns away sharply to exit. Motion lines suggest haste.

bubbles.text: You have said enough, madam. I have now only to be ashamed of my own feelings.

panels.description: Elizabeth sitting alone on a chair in the now-empty room, head in hands, crying, with a contemplative expression as the gravity of the confession sinks in. Dramatic lighting from the window.

bubbles.text: That he should have been in love with me for so many months... it was almost incredible. Yet, it was gratifying.
